text.line: LOOKS LIKE WE'VE GOT THE CHECKLIST DONE, WE CAN PROBABLY HEAD TO THE BOARD AND CHECK WHAT WE NEED FOR THE NEXT FLOOR.
WELL, THAT WAS UNDERWHELMING. WE'RE DONE WITH THIS PART OF THE CHECKLIST I SUPPOSE, BUT LET'S GO TO THE CORRIDOR TO RECOLLECT AND CHECK IT ONE LAST TIME.

intro.line: YOU FIND YOURSELF IN A SMALL ROOM WITH A DOOR TO THE EAST AND A DOOR TO THE SOUTH.
YOU ARE IN A SMALL LOUNGE ROOM WITH A FEW CHAIRS AND A TABLE. A CHECKLIST SITS ON A CORKBOARD ON THE WALL.
YOU ARE IN A SPACIOUS CORRIDOR WITH LOFTY LIGHTS HANGING ABOVE. THERE IS AN ELEVATOR TO THE SOUTH AND A DOORWAY TO THE EAST AND WEST.
YOU FIND YOURSELF IN A LARGE ROOM WITH DOZENS OF BLINKING TERMINALS SURROUNDING YOU.
THE ROOM WHIRS WITH THE SOUND OF SEVERAL LARGE FILTRATION UNITS AROUND YOU, DUST HANGING IN THE AIR.
YOU ARE IN A BRIGHT ROOM FILLED WITH ELECTRICAL EQUIPMENT AND SEVERAL LARGE GENERATORS.
YOU ARE IN A SMALL STEEL ELEVATOR WITH THREE BUTTONS, ONE FOR EACH FLOOR OF THE FACILITY.
YOU ARE IN A SMALL STEEL ELEVATOR WITH THREE BUTTONS, ONE FOR EACH FLOOR OF THE FACILITY.
YOU ARE IN A COZY CORRIDOR, WITH DOORS ON BOTH SIDES AND THE ELEVATOR TO THE SOUTH.
YOU ENTER THE GAME ROOM, A LARGE SPACE WITH SEVERAL SHELVES OF BOARD GAMES, TABLES TO PLAY THEM ON, AND A POOL TABLE IN THE CENTER.
YOU ENTER THE GYM, A LARGE ROOM WITH SEVERAL PIECES OF EXERCISE EQUIPMENT LINING THE WALLS AND A SMALL WEIGHTLIFTING AREA IN THE CENTER.
YOU ENTER THE QUARTERS, A LARGE ROOM WITH DOZENS OF BUNKBEDS LINING THE WALLS.
YOU ENTER THE FOOD STORAGE ROOM, A LARGE ROOM WITH SHELVES LINING THE WALLS, COVERED TOP TO BOTTOM IN MRES AND DUST.
YOU ENTER AN EXTREMELY OVERGROWN JUNGLE OF A ROOM, WITH PLANTERS OVERFLOWING WITH VINES AND FRUIT.
YOU ENTER THE WATER FILTRATION ROOM, A LARGE ROOM WITH SEVERAL LARGE WATER TANKS LINING THE WALLS AND A FEW SMALL DESKS WITH MONITORS ON THEM.
YOU ENTER THE HOSPITAL WARD, A LARGE ROOM WITH SEVERAL COTS LINING THE WALLS AND A FEW SHELVES FILLED WITH MEDICAL EQUIPMENT.
YOU ARE IN A SMALL STEEL ELEVATOR WITH THREE BUTTONS, ONE FOR EACH FLOOR OF THE FACILITY.

text.line: THE ROOM IS LARGELY EMPTY, WITH A SINGLE DARK TERMINAL AND A THICK CABLE DISCONNECTED ON THE FLOOR.
THERE IS A MAP OF THE FACILITY ON THE WALL, SHOWING THE GENERAL LAYOUT OF THE FACILITY.
THERE IS A DOOR SOUTH LEADING TO THE FACILITY LOUNGE, AND A DOOR LEADING EAST TO A LARGE CORRIDOR.
A DETAILED MAP SHOWS THE GENERAL LAYOUT OF THE FACILITY, SPLIT INTO THREE FLOORS.
YOU ARE CURRENTLY IN ROOM A1 ON THE FIRST FLOOR.
PROCESSING DATA....
MAP DATA RETRIEVED SUCCESSFULLY.
A1: BOOT ROOM. A2: FACILITY LOUNGE. A3: FLOOR ONE CORRIDOR. A4: CENTRAL DATABASE. A5: AIR FILTRATION CENTER. A6: GENERATOR ROOM. A7: ELEVATOR.
THE CORKBOARD IS COVERED WITH NOTES AND MEMOS DETAILING OFFICE ETIQUETTE AND DAILY OPERATIONS, ALONGSIDE YOUR MONTHLY CHECKLIST.
A VENDING MACHINE SITS IN THE CORNER OF THE ROOM.
THERE IS A SMALL COFFEE MAKER ON THE TABLE, ALONGSIDE SEVERAL MAGAZINES.
THE TABLE IS COVERED IN A THICK LAYER OF DUST. SOMEONE SHOULD PROBABLY CLEAN THAT.
THE TABLE AND COFFEEMAKER ARE SPOTLESS. YOU PAT YOURSELF ON THE BACK.
THE CHAIRS ARE WORN AND TATTERED, WITH TEARS IN THE FABRIC AND EXPOSED FOAM.
THERE IS A DOOR LEADING NORTH BACK TO THE BOOT ROOM.
PROCESSING CHECKLIST DATA...
THE CHECKLIST IS THOROUGH AND DETAILED. BEST TO TAKE IT ONE FLOOR AT A TIME.
CURRENT FACILITY LOGS: FLOOR 1 STATUS
[ ] CLEAN FACILITY LOUNGE: PENDING
[X] CLEAN FACILITY LOUNGE: COMPLETE
[ ] ENSURE SYSTEMWIDE TERMINAL FUNCTIONALITY: PENDING
[X] ENSURE SYSTEMWIDE TERMINAL FUNCTIONALITY: COMPLETE
[ ] REPLACE AIR FILTERS / MONITOR OXYGEN: PENDING
[X] REPLACE AIR FILTERS / MONITOR OXYGEN: COMPLETE
[ ] MONITOR GENERATOR PERFORMANCE / BATTERY: PENDING
[X] MONITOR GENERATOR PERFORMANCE / BATTERY: COMPLETE
AFTER COMPLETION OF FIRST FLOOR DUTIES LOG PERFORMANCE INTO THE TERMINAL AND MOVE ON TO FLOOR TWO. THE REMAINDER OF THE CHECKLIST WILL BECOME AVAILABLE AS NEEDED.
YOU CAN RETURN TO THE CHECKLIST AT ANY POINT TO SEE YOUR PROGRESS.
YOU TAKE A QUICK GLANCE OVER THE LIST, CONFIRMING THAT ALL THE FIRST FLOOR DUTIES ARE DONE.

THEN YOU LOOK OVER THE DUTIES OF THE SECOND FLOOR. CLEANING THE GAME ROOM, CHANGING BEDLINENS, TAKING INVENTORY OF RATIONS, REFILLING BOTANICAL WATERING RESERVOIRS, CHANGING WATER FILTERS, TAKING STOCK OF MEDICAL SUPPLIES, AND ADMINISTERING MENTAL HEALTH BENCHMARKS TO THE STAFF. LONG NIGHT AHEAD OF YOU. GUESS IT'S TIME TO HEAD DOWN TO THE SECOND FLOOR.
THE VENDING MACHINE IS FULLY STOCKED, ALL CLEAR HERE.
NONE OF THEM LOOK PARTICULARLY APPETIZING ANYWAYS, TRUST ME NOT WORTH A DESCRIPTION.
YOU GRAB A NEARBY RAG AND THOROUGHLY WIPE THE TABLE AND COFFEE MAKER DOWN, ORGANIZING THE MAGAZINES INTO A STACK.
WHILE YOU'RE AT IT, YOU ALSO WIPE DOWN THE CHAIRS, REMOVING SOME OF THE DUST AND DEBRIS.
YOU GRAB A NEARBY BROOM AND SWEEP THE FLOOR, MAKING THE ROOM FEEL MUCH CLEANER A LITTLE MORE LIVABLE FOR THE STAFF.
A SENSE OF PRIDE WELLS UP IN YOU AS YOU STEP BACK AND ADMIRE YOUR WORK.
MUCH BETTER.
THE WALLS OF THE CORRIDOR ARE A STARK WHITE, WITH DOORS TO BOTH SIDES OF YOU AND AN ELEVATOR TO THE SOUTH. ON THE NORTH SIDE, THERE IS A LARGE WINDOW.

YOU APPROACH THE WINDOW AND LOOK OUT INTO THE DARKNESS BELOW. IT'S A BREATHTAKING SIGHT, A DEEP SILO STRETCHING FAR INTO THE VOID, DIM LIGHTS EMBOSSED WITHIN THE WALL GIVING YOU A SLIGHT GLIMPSE AT WHAT LIES WITHIN.
MISSILES. TOO MANY TO COUNT, STACKED SIDE BY SIDE LIKE OMINOUS SENTINELS. I SUPPOSE THAT ANSWERS WHAT THE FACILITY IS FOR I GUESS.
IT'S TRULY A MAGESTIC SIGHT THOUGH, REGARDLESS OF HOW UNNERVING IT IS. TRUST ME, IT WAS WORTH THE LOOK.
NOT TERRIBLY MUCH ELSE TO DESCRIBE HERE, THERE'S A CORRIDOR TO THE WEST AND A DOOR TO THE SOUTH. THE TERMINALS CONTINUE TO BLINK BLANKLY AT YOU, WAITING FOR SOME KIND OF INPUT.

YOU APPROACH THE NEAREST TERMINAL AND TAP AT IT, THE SCREEN BOUNCING TO LIFE SEEMINGLY ECSTATIC TO FINALLY RECIEVE SOME ATTENTION. 
AN INSIGNIA OF A STAR WITHIN A GEAR SPINS IN THE CORNER WHILE THE INTRO TEXT ROLLS OVER THE SCREEN.
K-LEVEL BIOS V.4.23 - INITIALIZING...

Hello brother and welcome to the Allied Front Operations System (c). 
Please login to continue to the primary interface.
YOU TYPE IN THE CREDENTIALS TO YOUR ACCOUNT LIKE SECOND NATURE, THE TERMINAL HESITATING FOR A MOMENT BEFORE PROCESSING YOUR REQUEST.
Welcome back [[FACILITY MAINTENANCE OPERATOR]], please select an option to perform your duties.
THE TERMINAL NOW DISPLAYS SEVERAL OPTIONS FOR YOU TO CHOOSE FROM, THE MOST INTERESTING BEING FACILITY HISTORY. PROBABLY WORTH A PEEK.
RUNNING SYSTEM DIAGNOSTICS...

[SYSTEM]: CHECKING CORE MEMORY... OK 
[SYSTEM]: CHECKING PROCESSOR FUNCTIONALITY... OK 
[SYSTEM]: CHECKING NETWORK CONNECTIVITY... ERROR 
NETWORK CONNECTION LOST. PLEASE SEND A MAINTENTANCE REQUEST TO YOUR NEAREST IT SPECIALIST. 
[SYSTEM]: SCANNING LOGIC GATES... OK 
[SYSTEM]: VALIDATING ROBOTICS WIRELESS CONNECTION... OK
[SYSTEM]: ENSURING SYSTEM RADIATION INTEGRITY... ERROR#&*$*@(&%&*@
 [SYSTEM]: DIAGNOSTICS COMPLETE. SYSTEMS WITHIN NORMAL PARAMETERS.
WELL, MILDLY CONCERNING BUT NOTHING TO WORRY ABOUT AS FAR AS OUR RESPONSIBILITIES GO. I THINK I'M SAFE TO CHECK THAT OFF THE LIST.
ACCESSING FACILITY STATUS REPORTS...
FACILITY STATUS REPORTS MISSING. PLEASE CONTACT YOUR LOCAL FACILITY MAINTENANCE OPERATOR TO PERFORM MONTHLY MANUAL FACILITY MAINTENANCE CHECK AND LOG THE RESULTS.
I SUPPOSE THAT WOULD BE US. BETTER GET BACK TO WORK THEN.
ACCESSING FACILITY HISTORY LOGS...

Test Site Chelbaskia-40 is a cutting edge nuclear testing facility storing ~30% of the Allied Forces' nuclear arsenal, making it an integral part of our defensive line against the Collective Front (ten-thousand curses be to their names). 
While proudly holding the mantle of first line of defense, Chelbaskia-40 is also a cornerstone of experimental research and development for the Allied Front, with a large focus on experimental botanics, high-density fission generators, cutting-edge robotics technology, and functioning as an experimental bunker in the case of nuclear warfare. 
In the case of complete separation from the external grid Chelbaskia-40 has the resources to last ~200 years, with our fission generators capable on running in lower power mode for up to two centuries. 
Chelbaskia-40 is a model example of the superior power of Allied Forces' technology, and I hope you and your brothers are proud to be working there.
WELL, A BIT DRY BUT INTERESTING NONETHELESS. NICE TO KNOW WHERE YOU'RE WORKING I GUESS. BACK TO THE GRINDSTONE.

SEVERAL LARGE AIR FILTRATION UNITS LINE THE FLOOR, HUMMING LOUDLY AS THEY OPERATE. AN INTERFACE IS VISIBLE ON THE WALL, PATIENTLY WAITING FOR INPUT. VENTS ACROSS THE WALL PUSH AIR THROUGH THE ROOM, CREATING A SLIGHT BREEZE. A SMALL STORAGE CLOSET LURKS IN THE CORNER, SEVERAL AIR FILTERS VISIBLY LINING THE SHELVES.
THE INTERFACE IS A SIMPLE SCREEN WITH A SMALL KEYBOARD HUNG UNDERNEATH IT. A FEW OPTIONS ARE DISPLAYED.

CHECK OXYGEN LEVELS 
CHECK FILTER STATUS 
VERIFY VENT AIRFLOW

FLOOR ONE: OXYGEN SATURATION AT 13%. ENSURE PROPER VENTILATION IS MAINTAINED AND AIR FILTERS ARE PROPERLY CYCLED. 
FLOOR TWO: OXYGEN SATURATION AT 27%. NO IMMEDIATE CONCERNS. 
FLOOR THREE: OXYGEN SATURATION AT 12%. ENSURE PROPER VENTILATION IS MAINTAINED AND FILTERS ARE PROPERLY CYCLED.

WELL, IT'S WITHIN BREATHABLE PARAMETERS BUT A LITTLE LOW ON ONE AND THREE. RUNNING A BIT OVER ON TWO THOUGH, AIRFLOW IS PROBABLY GETTING DIVERTED INCORRECTLY. BETTER CYCLE THOSE FILTERS AND CHECK THE VENTS.
WHATEVER THE OXYGEN SATURATION PROBLEM IS ISN'T THE FILTERS. WE'RE GONNA HAVE TO CHECK FLOOR TWO TO FIGURE OUT EXACTLY WHAT'S GOING ON.
IT HAS BEEN [30] DAYS SINCE THE LAST FILTER REPLACEMENT. PLEASE REPLACE FILTER IMMEDIATELY.
NOTHING WE DIDN'T ALREADY KNOW. TIME TO GET ON IT.
IT HAS BEEN [0 DAYS] SINCE THE LAST FILTER REPLACEMENT. NEXT FILTER REPLACEMENT DUE IN [30 DAYS].
I'VE BEEN WORKIN', BEEN WORKIN', BUT I GOT ME SO TERRIBLE LONG TO GO.
RUNNING VENTILATION DIAGNOSTICS...
YOU HEAR THE VENTS WHIRR EVEN HARDER, AND WITHIN THAT WHIRRING YOU CAN BARELY MAKE OUT A KIND OF CHOKING SOUND. NOT A GOOD SIGN.
THE VENT AIRFLOW IS REDUCED BY 20%. PRIMARY RESISTANCE POINT IDENTIFIED ON FLOOR TWO. FIBROUS ORGANIC MATERIAL DETECTED. PLEASE RESOLVE BLOCKAGE IMMEDIATELY.
ANOTHER THING TO ADD TO THE NEVER-ENDING LIST. WE'LL GIVE IT A LOOK AFTER FINISHING THIS FLOOR.
YOU OPEN THE DOORS TO THE STORAGE CLOSET, REVEALING SEVERAL SHELVES LINED WITH GOVERNMENT ISSUED AIR FILTERS. YOU GRAB A HANDFUL OFF THE SHELF.
YOU APPROACH THE VENTS HANGING ABOVE YOU AND LISTEN CLOSELY. THE WHIRRING SOUND IS SLIGHTLY CLUNKIER THAN USUAL, DOESN'T SEEM TO BE WORKING RIGHT.

YOU TAKE THE AIR FILTERS YOU GRABBED FROM THE STORAGE CLOSET AND BEGIN REPLACING THEM FROM EACH UNIT, GOING ONE BY ONE DOWN THE LINE. 
FIRST YOU GRAB THE POP-OFF CORNERS OF THE FILTRATION PANELS, PULLING OUT DUSTY AND TANGLED AIR FILTERS AND REPLACING EACH ONE WITH PRECISION AND CARE. 
IT'S TEDIOUS WORK AND TIME CONSUMING, BUT IT'S IMPORTANT FOR THE HEALTH OF THE CREW. 
FINALLY, YOU POP THE LAST PANEL BACK ON AND STEP BACK. FILTER REPLACEMENT COMPLETE. GREAT JOB TEAM.

DOZENS OF GENERATORS ARE PLACED AROUND THE ROOM. ALL BUT ONE SIT SILENT, WITH A TERMINAL ON THE WALL DISPLAYING THE STATUS OF THE SLEEPING BEHEMOTHS. VARIOUS ELECTRICAL CABLES AND WIRES COVER THE FLOOR AND SNAKE UP THE WALLS, HEADING OFF TO OTHER ROOMS TO PERFORM THEIR TASKS.
THE TERMINAL DISPLAYS THE STATUS OF THE GENERATORS- AH SHIT.

GENERATOR 1: 0% 
GENERATOR 2: 0%
GENERATOR 3: 0%
GENERATOR 4: 0%
 GENERATOR 5: 0%
 GENERATOR 6: 0%
THIS CONTINUES AS YOU'D EXPECT IT TO. LET'S JUST CUT AHEAD.

GENERATOR 23: 0% 
GENERATOR 24: 0% 
GENERATOR 25: 18% 

TOTAL FACILITY POWER: 0.72% 
TIME UNTIL FACILITY SHUTDOWN: 18 MONTHS
MORE TIME THAN I THOUGH, LESS THAN I'D HOPE. HERE'S HOPING A TECHNICIAN FIGURES THAT OUT BECAUSE I DON'T THINK THAT'S UNDER OUR JOB DESCRIPTION.

YOU WALK AROUND THE ROOM, HOVERING OVER EACH GENERATOR. ALMOST ALL SIT COLD AND DEAD EXCEPT FOR ONE STILL SPUTTERING ALONG. 
HOLD ON LITTLE GUY, HELP'S GOTTA BE COMING SOON.

THE ELEVATOR IS A COLD, CLINICAL STEEL BOX WITH A SINGLE LIGHTBULB HANGING FROM THE CEILING. THERE'S THREE BUTTONS VISIBLE ON THE BOARD, ONE, TWO AND THREE. THE DISPLAY AT THE TOP OF THE ELEVATOR READS 1.
HEY DUMBASS THAT'S THE FLOOR WE'RE ON NOW.

YOU PRESS THE BUTTON FOR THE SECOND FLOOR, THE ELEVATOR MAKING A CONCERINING CLANKING SOUND AS YOU FEEL IT DESCEND 
AFTER WHAT FEELS LIKE AN ETERNITY THE DOOR OPENS UP IN FRONT OF YOU, SHOWING ANOTHER CORRIDOR NOT DISSIMILAR FROM THE ONE YOU JUST CAME FROM.
WE'RE GONNA NEED TO FINISH THE CHECKLIST FIRST BEFORE GOING ANYWHERE ELSE.
LET'S CLEAR OUT FLOOR TWO BEFORE WE HEAD DOWN TO THREE.
CURRENT FACILITY LOGS: FLOOR 2 STATUS
[ ] CLEAN GAME ROOM: PENDING
[X] CLEAN GAME ROOM: COMPLETE
[ ] CHANGE BEDLINENS: PENDING
[X] CHANGE BEDLINENS: COMPLETE
[ ] TAKE INVENTORY OF RATIONS: PENDING
[X] TAKE INVENTORY OF RATIONS: COMPLETE
[ ] REFILL BOTANICAL WATERING RESERVOIRS: PENDING
[X] REFILL BOTANICAL WATERING RESERVOIRS: COMPLETE
[ ] CHANGE WATER FILTERS: PENDING
[X] CHANGE WATER FILTERS: COMPLETE
[ ] TAKE STOCK OF MEDICAL SUPPLIES: PENDING
[X] TAKE STOCK OF MEDICAL SUPPLIES: COMPLETE
[ ] ADMINISTER MENTAL HEALTH BENCHMARKS TO STAFF: PENDING
[X] ADMINISTER MENTAL HEALTH BENCHMARKS TO STAFF: COMPLETE
AFTER WE FINISH THESE WE CAN GO FINISH UP FLOOR THREE. THEN WE DO WHATEVER WE DO AFTER THAT.
You can check the checklist at any point from the corridor.
YOU TAKE A QUICK GLANCE OVER THE LIST, CONFIRMING THAT ALL THE SECOND FLOOR DUTIES ARE DONE.

I DON'T KNOW IF YOU REALIZED, BUT THERE'S NO ONE HERE. AT ALL. GHOST TOWN. 
WHAT THE HELL ARE WE DOING HERE? WHERE IS EVERYONE? HOW LONG HAS IT REALLY BEEN DOWN HERE? 
THE GENERATORS HAVE BEEN RUNNING FOR GOD KNOWS HOW LONG, THE RATIONS ARE UNTOUCHED, NO ONE'S BEEN IN ANY OF THE BEDS, AND THE PLANTS HAVE GONE CRAZY AND OVERTAKEN THE ENTIRE BOTANICAL DEPARTMENT. 
I THINK WE'RE ALONE DOWN HERE.
WHAT ARE WE DOING DOWN HERE?
I'M GONNA NEED SOME TIME TO THINK. LET'S JUST GET TO THE THIRD FLOOR.

THE ELEVATOR IS A COLD, CLINICAL STEEL BOX WITH A SINGLE LIGHTBULB HANGING FROM THE CEILING. THERE'S THREE BUTTONS VISIBLE ON THE BOARD, ONE, TWO AND THREE. THE DISPLAY AT THE TOP OF THE ELEVATOR READS 2.
ALREADY CLEARED OUT THAT FLOOR. NO NEED TO VISIT IT AGAIN.
GENIUS, MAYBE THERE'S A secret FLOOR BY PRESSING THE SAME BUTTON TWICE.
LET'S CLEAR OUT FLOOR TWO BEFORE WE HEAD BACK DOWN TO THREE.

YOU PRESS THE BUTTON FOR THE THIRD FLOOR, THE ELEVATOR CLUNKING ALONG AS YOU'RE BROUGHT DEEPER AND DEEPER INTO THE FACILITY 

YOU KNOW I'VE BEEN THINKING 

WHY ARE WE STILL PIDDLING AROUND WITH MONTHLY MAINTENANCE FOR A FACILITY THAT'S CLEARLY LONG ABANDONED? 

AFTER WE FINISH THIS UP, LET'S LOOK AT GETTING OUT OF HERE.
THE ELEVATOR DOOR OPENS. WE'RE HERE, LAST TREK.

THE CORRIDOR IS A LONG ORNATE HALLWAY WITH WARM LIGHTING AND DOORS LINING THE EAST AND WEST, ALONGSIDE AN ELEVATOR AT THE SOUTH END. THERE'S A LARGE MAP OF THE FACILITY ON THE WALL, SHOWING THE LAYOUT OF THE ROOMS. 
YOU ALSO RECOLLECT THE CHECKLIST FOR THIS FLOOR.
A DETAILED MAP SHOWS THE GENERAL LAYOUT OF THE FACILITY, SPLIT INTO THREE FLOORS.
YOU ARE CURRENTLY IN ROOM B1 ON THE SECOND FLOOR.
PROCESSING DATA....
MAP DATA RETRIEVED SUCCESSFULLY.
B1: SECOND FLOOR CORRIDOR. B2: GAME ROOM. B3: QUARTERS. B4: FOOD STORAGE. B5: BOTANICALS. B6: WATER FILTRATION. B7: GYM. B8: HOSPITAL WARD. B9: ELEVATOR.

YOU TAKE A LOOK AROUND THE SPACIOUS ROOM AND TAKE INVENTORY, MAKING NOTE OF THE SEVERAL BRANDS OF BOARD GAMES ON THE SHELVES, THE FRAYED POOL TABLE WITH THE BALLS NEATLY ARRANGED IN A TRIANGLE, AND A BROOM AND MOP TUCKED INTO THE CORNER.

YOU RIFLE THROUGH A FEW OF THE BOARD GAMES, TAKING NOTE OF THE OVERT PROPAGANDA THEMING ACROSS ALL OF THEM. 
"SMASH THE PIG", "SECRET INFILTRATION", "ALLIED FORCES-OPOLY" (OH COME ON THEY'RE NOT EVEN TRYING TO BE SUBTLE WITH THAT ONE). 
YOU OPEN THE LAST ONE, READING THROUGH THE RULES. LOOKS LIKE A TWIST ON TYPICAL PROPERTY MANAGEMENT GAMES WHERE YOU TRY TO BALANCE GETTING PROPERTIES ALONG WITH AVOIDING THE EYE OF THE STATE, WHICH INCURS HARSH PENALTIES FOR OWNERSHIP OF LAND. YOU SKIP TO THE END OF THE RULES AND MAKE NOTE OF AN ENDGAME EVENT WHERE ANYONE WITH PROPERTY EXCEEDING THE GOVERNMENT-SANCTIONED ALLOTMENT IS IMMEDIATELY DISQUALIFIED AND SHOULD BE REPORTED TO THEIR SUPERIORS IN REAL LIFE. 
A TAD BLEAK, BUT COULD BE FUN.
THE POOL TABLE IS IN DECENT CONDITION, THE CLOTH A LITTLE FRAYED BUT THE BALLS ARE ALL THERE AND IN GOOD SHAPE. NOT BAD FOR A FACILITY THIS OLD.
YOU GRAB A NEARBY RAG AND THOROUGHLY WIPE THE POOL TABLE DOWN, REMOVING SOME OF THE DUST AND DEBRIS. UNFORTUNATELY SOME OF THE LINING COMES WITH IT.
THEN YOU SWEEP THE FLOOR, MAKING THE ROOM FEEL MUCH CLEANER.
NOT QUITE AS SATISFYING AS THE LAST ONE. DOESN'T LOOK LIKE ANYONE'S USED THE SPACE IN A WHILE.
WORK IS WORK I GUESS, TIME TO MOVE ON.
YOU TAKE STOCK OF THE PLENTIFUL BUNKBEDS AROUND THE ROOM, PROBABLY AROUND FORTY IN TOTAL.
CHANGING ALL OF THESE LINENS IS GOING TO BE A PAIN IN THE ASS.
CHANGING ALL OF THESE LINENS WAS A MASSIVE PAIN IN THE ASS. HOPE WHATEVER INVISIBLE PEOPLE ARE IN CHARGE OF THIS PLACE SLEEP WELL, ROTTEN BASTARDS.

YOU BEGIN THE ENDLESSLY TEDIOUS TASK OF CHANGING ALL OF THE LINENS, TAKING DOZENS OF TRIPS BACK AND FORTH FROM THE STORAGE ROOM IN THE BACK OF THE QUARTERS TO GRAB THE NECESSARY SUPPLIES, WHICH APPEAR TO BE IN SHORT SUPPLY. YOU BARELY SCROUNGE UP ENOUGH SHEETS AND COMFORTERS TO CHANGE ALL OF THE BEDS, LEAVING A SMALL HANDFUL LEFT AFTER GRABBING ALL OF THE NECESSARY ONES. 
YOU TAKE THE OLD LINENS AND DISPOSE OF THEM IN THE CLEANING BASKET, DEAR LORD SOMEONE NEEDS TO EMPTY THIS THING THERE HAS GOT TO BE HUNDREDS OF OLD SHEETS IN HERE. GUESS WHOEVER IS ON LAUNDRY DUTY TOOK OFF AT SOME POINT. 
AFTER WALKING BACK INTO THE ROOM TO ADMIRE YOUR WORK YOU REALIZE THAT ALL OF THE BEDS WERE PERFECTLY MADE WHEN YOU CAME IN HERE, IF A BIT DUSTY. STRANGE.

THE FOOD STORAGE ROOM IS A LARGE WAREHOUSE-STYLE ROOM WITH DOZENS OF SHELVES STACKING UP TO THE CEILING, ALL ORGANIZED AND WELL-STOCKED. 
THERE'S A SMALL TABLE AT THE FORE OF THE ROOM WITH A CLIPBOARD ON IT WHERE YOU APPARENTLY ARE SUPPOSED TO WRITE DOWN THE INVENTORY.

THE CLIPBOARD HAS A SERIES OF FIELDS FOR TAKING INVENTORY AND THE DATE, STRETCHING BACK FOR MONTHS ALL THE WAY TO THE BEGINNING OF THE PAGE. 
TWENTY-TWO MONTHS IN TOTAL ACTUALLY, WITH TWO MORE SLOTS REMAINING. YOU TAKE A QUICK GLANCE THROUGH THE BOXES.

SEPT | 996 TONNES | -0 KG | STABLE 
OCT | 996 TONNES | -0 KG | STABLE 
NOV | 996 TONNES | -0 KG | STABLE 
DEC | 996 TONNES | -0 KG | STABLE 
JAN | 996 TONNES | -0 KG | STABLE 
FEB | 996 TONNES | -0 KG | STABLE 
MAR | 996 TONNES | -0 KG | STABLE 
APR | 996 TONNES | -0 KG | STABLE 
MAY | 996 TONNES | -0 KG | STABLE 
JUN | 996 TONNES | -0 KG | STABLE 
JUL | 996 TONNES | -0 KG | STABLE 
AUG | 996 TONNES | -0 KG | STABLE 
SEP | 996 TONNES | -0 KG | STABLE 
OCT| 996 TONNES |-0 KG| STABLE
NOV| 996 TONNES |-0 KG| STABLE
DEC| 996 TONNES |-0 KG| STABLE
JAN| 996 TONNES |-0 KG| STABLE
FEB| 996 TONNES |-0 KG| STABLE
MAR| 996 TONNES |-0 KG| STABLE
APR| 996 TONNES |-0 KG| STABLE
MAY| 996 TONNES |-0 KG| STABLE
JUN| 996 TONNES |-0 KG| STABLE
JUL| 996 TONNES |-0 KG| STABLE
WELL WE CERTAINLY AREN'T STARVING ANYTIME SOON.

YOU TAKE A WALK AROUND THE ROOM, CHECKING OFF EACH ITEM ON THE CLIPBOARD AS YOU CONFIRM THAT IT'S FULLY STOCKED. 
AFTER TAKING STOCK OF EVERYTHING YOU REALIZE THAT EVERYTHING IS IN ORDER, WITH NO ISSUES TO REPORT. NOT BAD.
